title: niphobolus drakeanus
intro: Niphobolus drakeanus, also known as Maidenhair Fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dryopteridaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is rich in saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and providing relief from respiratory problems. Niphobolus drakeanus is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and in some cases, it is also used in the form of a paste or ointment for topical application.
